content.full_text:
Letter from Jerry W. Hill, Assistant Secretary of State to Carla Wardlow, Assistant Chief, Fair Political Practices Commission

Date: September 11, 1992

Subject: Advice on Employee Attendance at Federal Trademark Training Classes

---

March Fong Eu
Secretary of State
1230 J Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Ms. Carla Wardlow, Assistant Chief
Fair Political Practices Commission
Technical Assistance Division
421 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Ms. Wardlow:

The Secretary of State's Office (SOS) is requesting advice on the following matter:

At the expense of the State Bar Association, is it permissible under the provisions of the Political Reform Act, for a SOS employee to attend work related (trademark filing) training classes conducted by the federal government?

Currently, the California Trademark Office is adopting the federal standards used in reviewing and filing trademark documents. Charlene Culbertson, a Legal Document Examiner (senior trademark examiner) has primary responsibility for the on-going process and success of this major project. Ms. Culbertson is tentatively scheduled to attend classes conducted by the U.S. Patent and Trademark Office. Ms. Culbertson's participation in such training will provide a greater understanding of the legal reasoning applied by the examining staff at the federal level. Classes are scheduled for winter 1993 and will cover subjects such as ornamentation, configuration, trade names, disclaimers and mark usage. These classes are generally conducted to benefit new examining attorneys.

The anticipated cost for this trip is approximately $2,000 and the State Bar Association has offered to sponsor Ms. Culbertson's training and travel expenses.

If you have any questions, please contact me at 445-6375.

Sincerely,

JERRY W. HILL
Assistant Secretary of State
